sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer, Inc. - Supermicro Brings Superior Performance and Efficiency to AI at the Edge
feed.summary: Supermicro is expanding its range of AI-optimized servers, introducing new systems for edge and embedded workloads that leverage Intel Xeon 6 and Intel Core Ultra processors. These new servers are designed to deliver enhanced performance, efficiency, and real-time AI inferencing capabilities from the data center to the far edge. The offerings include solutions for telecom, networking, IoT, and enterprise edge use cases, supporting higher core counts, increased memory bandwidth, and integrated AI acceleration.

feed.title: AMD FSR 3 Frame Generation Slow Level Loading Fix _Marvel Rivals - The Super Hero Team-based PVP Shooter
feed.summary: Marvel Rivals has identified an issue where AMD FSR 3 Frame Generation causes slow level loading times on devices with fewer CPU threads. A fix for this problem is scheduled to be deployed in an upcoming update on March 13. The company reassures players of their commitment to an epic gaming experience and provides support contact information.

feed.title: SanDisk to Hike NAND Prices by Over 10% as US Memory Giant Expects Supply to Tighten
feed.summary: SanDisk, a leading memory chip maker, plans to increase its NAND flash memory prices by over 10% from April 1st in anticipation of a supply shortage. This decision follows production cuts by major OEMs due to a previous surplus, and while it signals a potential market recovery, its acceptance by clients and the impact of weak demand in consumer electronics remain uncertain. The long-term outlook depends on the recovery of PC and smartphone demand and the adoption of AI features in gadgets.

feed.title: Intel appoints ex-Cadence CEO amid turnaround struggles
feed.summary: Intel has appointed Lip-Bu Tan, former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, as its new CEO to lead the company's turnaround efforts, succeeding interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus. Tan is tasked with revitalizing the struggling chipmaker and transforming it into a contract manufacturer, leveraging his extensive experience in the semiconductor industry. Analysts believe this appointment signals Intel's commitment to keeping its design and foundry businesses integrated, though challenges remain given Intel's recent struggles in market share and emerging tech trends.

feed.title: Intel finally gets a new CEO: industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan
feed.summary: Intel has appointed industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO, hoping to reverse a years-long decline and regain market share. Tan, previously CEO of Cadence Design Systems and a former Intel board member, plans for the company to double down on its strengths and take calculated risks in areas where it lags. This appointment comes after a period of leadership changes and financial challenges for Intel, with analysts offering mixed reactions but mostly acknowledging the need for a strategic turnaround.

feed.title: Block Becomes First Company in North America to Deploy the Latest NVIDIA GB200 Systems for Frontier Models
feed.summary: Block, Inc. announced it will be the first company in North America to deploy the NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with DGX GB200 systems, housed in an Equinix data center. This infrastructure will support Block's research and training of open-source generative AI models, aiming to advance AI in underexplored areas. The initiative underscores Block's commitment to open-source innovation and solving real-world problems for its customers and community, building on its previous work in deepfake detection and hyper-realistic generated audio.

feed.title: Newegg Launches the World’s Most Advanced Gaming CPU: The AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D
feed.summary: Newegg has launched the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D and AMD Ryzen 9 9900X3D processors, which are now available for purchase. These CPUs are touted as the world's most advanced gaming desktop processors, featuring high core/thread counts, significant cache, and AMD's Zen 5 architecture. Newegg also offers compatible AM5 motherboards, pre-built gaming PCs with these processors, and an expanded CPU Trade-In Program to facilitate upgrades.

feed.title: Synopsys SNUG Silicon Valley 2025 to Feature Arm, OpenAI and Microsoft Executive Talks
feed.summary: Synopsys will host its annual SNUG Silicon Valley conference on March 19-20, 2025, featuring keynotes from Synopsys CEO Sassine Ghazi, Arm CEO Rene Haas, and OpenAI's Head of Hardware Richard Ho, with a virtual appearance by Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella. The event will explore the future of AI and intelligent systems, alongside an inaugural, invite-only Synopsys Executive Forum for industry leaders. The conference aims to foster collaboration and share best practices in chip and system design, covering topics like AI, 3D design, and software-defined systems.

feed.title: Intel found someone brave enough to be its new CEO—but is Lip-Bu Tan a change maker or more of the same?
feed.summary: Intel has appointed Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO, a move that prompted a 10% jump in stock price. Tan, a semiconductor industry veteran and former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, brings a unique blend of design and manufacturing expertise. However, the author questions if he will truly shake up Intel's long-standing ambivalence regarding its identity and strategic direction, or if his appointment is merely a safe "Goldilocks" choice that attempts to please everyone without committing to radical change.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE) and SentinelOne (S)
feed.summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for technology companies Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE), and SentinelOne (S). Intel received a "Hold" rating from TD Cowen and an upgrade to "Hold" from Bank of America Securities, with price targets indicating a modest upside. Adobe and SentinelOne both received "Buy" ratings from analysts at BMO Capital and Loop Capital Markets, respectively, with their price targets suggesting significant upside potential for investors.

feed.title: Dell’s new RTX Pro AI PC boasts an ‘unlimited turbo’ mode
feed.summary: Dell has announced its new "Pro Max" lineup of workstation desktops and laptops, featuring Nvidia's RTX Pro Blackwell GPUs and an innovative "unlimited turbo" mode for sustained high performance in its Tower T2 desktop. This series targets AI developers and power users, offering advanced cooling, large displays, and significant memory and storage capacities, including options with tandem OLED screens and modular USB-C ports. The range will include both Intel Core Ultra Series 2 and AMD Threadripper CPUs, with some models shipping in March and others in July.

feed.title: SoftBank agrees to buy Arm chipmaker Ampere Computing for $6.5B
feed.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. announced its acquisition of Arm chipmaker Ampere Computing LLC for $6.5 billion, with the deal expected to close in the second half of the year. Ampere will operate as an independent subsidiary, continuing its focus on Arm-based CPUs for data center servers, including its new AmpereOne M chip and the upcoming Aurora processor. SoftBank Chairman Masayoshi Son stated that the acquisition is driven by the need for breakthrough computing power for "artificial super intelligence," further deepening SoftBank's commitment to AI innovation in the United States and complementing its existing stake in Arm Holdings Plc.

feed.title: Outlook 2025: GenAI to Push Semiconductor Industry Towards a New Growth Phase
feed.summary: Generative AI is expected to be the main growth driver for the semiconductor industry in 2025 by increasing demand for advanced computational power. This will lead to new market opportunities in AI-enabled devices across various sectors and drive in-house chip design and specialized, energy-efficient AI hardware for edge deployments. The industry faces challenges in energy consumption and model compatibility, requiring collaboration and standardized frameworks.

feed.title: How Will FactSet Stock React To Its Upcoming Earnings?
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) is scheduled to report earnings, with analysts predicting a slight decline in EPS to $4.18 and a 4.5% revenue increase to $571 million. The article analyzes historical post-earnings stock reactions, showing positive one-day returns 50% of the time over the past five years, with a median positive return of 3.8% and a median negative return of -4.3%. It also explores correlations between short-term and medium-term returns and the influence of peer performance.

feed.title: 4 Best Stocks To Buy Under $5
feed.summary: This article highlights four promising stocks trading under $5: POET Technologies, TeraWulf, Ares Commercial Real Estate, and Regulus Therapeutics. Each company is presented with potential growth catalysts, including advancements in AI, a recovering Bitcoin market, strong real estate sectors, and positive clinical trial results and insider buying, respectively. The author emphasizes that low-priced stocks can offer significant returns if they have a compelling story or game-changing technology.

feed.title: Should You Buy Vertex Pharmaceuticals Stock At $510?
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) stock has seen a significant 27% surge year-to-date, contrasting sharply with the S&P 500's decline, driven by FDA approval of its new non-opioid pain reliever, Journavx. Despite its attractive historical performance and strong fundamentals across growth, financial stability, and downturn resilience, the stock is currently valued very highly, making it a volatile and tricky investment. The article suggests that while there's minimal cause for concern regarding its operating performance and financial health, its high valuation warrants cautious consideration.

feed.title: Nvidia unveils next generation of DGX SuperPOD in partnership with Equinix
feed.summary: Nvidia has unveiled its next generation of DGX SuperPOD, the DGX GB300 SuperPOD, designed to enhance AI inferencing with Grace Blackwell Architecture. This release comes as Nvidia partners with Equinix Inc., which will manage Nvidia's Instant AI Factory services, providing ready-made, AI-ready data centers globally. The collaboration aims to democratize AI deployment, enabling organizations to quickly scale and operate advanced AI infrastructure.

feed.title: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. Provides Business Update and Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
feed.summary: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. announced its "202 5G CT" program, signaling the "Year of 5G" with forthcoming 5G chipset shipments in the first half of 2025. The company reported a net loss of $5.0 million for Q4 2024 and $12.4 million for the full year 2024, emphasizing a shift towards 5G chipset sales for future growth. GCT Semiconductor also highlighted significant debt reduction and ongoing discussions for capital funding.

feed.title: Accelerating the Future of Transportation with SES AI’s NVIDIA-Powered Innovation for Electric Vehicles
feed.summary: SES AI is utilizing NVIDIA hardware and software, including domain-adapted large language models (LLMs) and GPU-accelerated simulations, to rapidly discover new battery materials for electric vehicles. This collaboration has led to significant advancements, such as a 1.6Kx acceleration in simulation workflows, a 25% increase in LLM accuracy, and the identification of 17 candidate materials, resulting in a 20% improvement in battery cycle life. Their approach aims to reduce the time for battery electrolyte discovery from five years to six months, driving sustainable transportation.

feed.title: Shopify Takes Steps To Pull In Billions From Passive Funds
feed.summary: Shopify is moving its US-listed shares from the New York Stock Exchange to the Nasdaq Global Select Market, starting March 31. This strategic move could allow the e-commerce company to be included in major stock indexes like the Nasdaq 100, attracting significant investment from passively managed funds. While inclusion can increase buying power, it also ties stock performance more closely to the broader market, making it a "two-way street" according to analysts.

feed.title: SentinelOne CEO Tomer Weingarten sells $1.17 million in stock
feed.summary: SentinelOne (NYSE: S) CEO Tomer Weingarten recently sold 60,864 shares of the company's Class A common stock for approximately $1.17 million. This transaction was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, leaving his direct ownership at 920,297 shares, despite also acquiring shares through conversions and exercised options without cash exchange. The sale comes amid varied analyst responses to SentinelOne's Q4 results and future guidance, leading to adjusted price targets from several firms.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Can Double in Price: Here’s Why
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is projected to double in price due to its increasing market share against NVIDIA in GPU and CPU markets, driven by lower-cost, better-performing products. Insider transactions, strong institutional buying, and an undervalued stock price relative to its growth potential support this optimistic outlook. The company is actively addressing its software infrastructure gap to further compete with NVIDIA.

feed.title: Qualcomm Stock Is Coiling for a Breakout
feed.summary: Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM) is showing signs of an impending breakout, supported by robust technicals, an attractive valuation, and bullish analyst targets. The stock has consistently bounced off its key $150 support level, indicating strong buying interest after recent declines. With diversification efforts in automotive and AI chips paying off, Qualcomm offers significant value compared to its semiconductor peers.

feed.title: Axsome’s latest win comes in ADHD, Opthea seeks path after AMD miss, and more: Clinical Report
feed.summary: Axsome Therapeutics has achieved a clinical success with its drug solriamfetol, which met the primary endpoint in a Phase III study for ADHD. The drug, a selective dopamine and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor, demonstrated significant improvement in symptom scores compared to placebo. This win comes as Axsome prepares a regulatory submission in Alzheimer's disease, while Opthea navigates challenges after a miss in Age-related Macular Degeneration.

feed.title: Supermicro Ships Over 20 New Systems that Redefine Single-Socket Performance and Deliver Data Center Power, Space, and Cost Savings
feed.summary: Supermicro announced the availability of over 20 new single-socket server systems supporting Intel Xeon 6 with P-Cores, designed to reduce data center power, space, and cost. These systems offer performance previously requiring dual-socket configurations, providing advantages like increased core counts, faster memory, and enhanced PCIe 5.0 lanes for diverse workloads. The new servers aim to optimize TCO and environmental impact across various applications from cloud computing to AI.

feed.title: US firm Vishay Intertechnology confirms £250m investment at UK's biggest semiconductor facility
feed.summary: US tech company Vishay Intertechnology has announced a £250m investment at its Newport foundry in South Wales, which will focus on producing advanced silicon carbide semiconductors for electric vehicles. This investment is part of Vishay's larger plan to inject £1bn into the facility by 2030, creating hundreds of new high-skilled jobs and potentially expanding into defense, aerospace, and renewable energy sectors. The UK Government is supporting this through its Automotive Transformation Fund, aiming to boost the economy and position the UK as a leader in advanced semiconductors.

feed.title: SOXL stock: More chaos to come ahead of ‘Liberation Day’?
feed.summary: The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares (SOXL) ETF has notably declined due to macroeconomic uncertainties and anxiety over President Trump’s tariff agenda. Investors are monitoring April 2, dubbed "Liberation Day," for new tariff announcements that could further impact semiconductor demand, even if the sector avoids direct tariffs, particularly through collateral damage to chip-consuming industries like automotive. The future of the CHIPS Act also remains a wildcard, despite bipartisan support.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD): Among Billionaire Ken Fisher’s Top Growth Stock Picks
feed.summary: Billionaire Ken Fisher, through Fisher Investments, holds a significant stake in Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), making it one of his top growth stock picks. AMD's strong performance is attributed to increasing demand for its CPUs and GPUs in data centers, especially within the booming AI segment. Fisher Asset Management's investment strategy focuses on growth stocks, and AMD's robust revenue and free cash flow growth in 2024 underscore its potential.

feed.title: Game-Changing News for Advanced Micro Devices
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is integrating Rapt.AI into its MI300X, MI325X, and upcoming MI350 GPUs, which is expected to enhance its AI semiconductor capabilities and market share. The company also completed the acquisition of ZT Systems to offer end-to-end AI infrastructure solutions. Despite a recent stock price correction, analysts are still bullish on AMD, seeing it as a deep-value opportunity with significant growth catalysts.

feed.title: Deciphering the shift from conversational to embodied AI
feed.summary: The article discusses the evolution of AI from conversational models like ChatGPT to embodied intelligence, which involves AI agents interacting with the physical world through robotic bodies. It highlights the potential impact of embodied AI on various industries and daily life, as well as the challenges and advancements in China's efforts to develop this technology. Key points include the establishment of innovation centers and new platforms to accelerate research and application of embodied AI.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries, Taiwan's UMC Rumored to Be Investigating a Merger to Build a US Chipmaker
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries and Taiwan's UMC are reportedly considering a merger to create a US-based chipmaker. This move aims to navigate potential trade wars and rising tariffs, leveraging GlobalFoundries' US presence and "trusted foundry" status. The combined entity would operate fabs across the US, Asia, and Europe, addressing increasing demand for localized chip production and reducing reliance on foreign companies amidst a growing independent semiconductor industry in China.

feed.title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF During the Nasdaq Correction? History Offers a Clear Answer.

It highlights the ETF's exposure to top AI and tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia, and several less-noticed AI powerhouses. The author points out that despite current economic uncertainties and tariffs contributing to the correction, the Nasdaq-100 has historically recovered to new highs after every downturn since 1985.

Kaynes also has plans for expansion, including a new PCB factory and future projects in other states.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer, Inc. - Industry's First-to-Market Supermicro NVIDIA HGX™ B200 Systems Demonstrate AI Performance Leadership on MLPerf® Inference v5.0 Results
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. announced leading performance on MLPerf Inference v5.0 benchmarks using their NVIDIA HGX B200 8-GPU systems, demonstrating over three times the token generation per second for Llama2-70B and Llama3.1-405B compared to previous generations. Both liquid-cooled and air-cooled systems achieved top results, showcasing Supermicro's innovation and collaboration with NVIDIA in AI workloads. The company's optimized systems were operational and available to customers before the benchmark start date, with results validated by MLCommons as reproducible.

feed.title: Qualcomm seeks AI expansion with possible Alphawave buy
feed.summary: Qualcomm is looking to expand its presence in the rapidly growing AI market by potentially acquiring UK-based semiconductor maker Alphawave IP Group and has already acquired MovianAI, VinAI's generative AI division. These moves aim to diversify Qualcomm's portfolio beyond mobile phone chipsets, whose revenue growth is slowing, and to strengthen its AI infrastructure offerings to compete with rivals like Nvidia, Intel, and AMD. The company faces urgency in diversification due to Apple phasing out Qualcomm modems in iPhones.

feed.title: Newegg announces reverse stock split to maintain Nasdaq listing
feed.summary: Newegg Commerce, Inc. announced a twenty-to-one reverse stock split, effective April 7, 2025, to meet Nasdaq's minimum price per share requirement, as its stock trades at $0.27 and has declined nearly 32% year-to-date. The company's financial health is rated as weak by InvestingPro, with concerns in profitability and price momentum, despite the company also recently expanding its product lineup with new AMD and NVIDIA GPUs. The split will proportionately increase the par value of shares and fractional shares will be exchanged for cash.

feed.title: US Computer Vision in Healthcare Market worth $11.53 billion by 2029
feed.summary: The US Computer Vision in Healthcare Market is projected to reach USD 11.53 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 24.0% from 2024, driven by the need for efficient medical imaging analysis and AI-driven solutions. PC-based computer vision systems currently hold the largest market share due to their affordability and integration capabilities, while patient activity monitoring and fall prevention are the fastest-growing segments. Predictive maintenance applications also account for a significant share, supported by AI and machine learning advancements.

feed.title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta’s latest Llama models put open-source AI in the driving seat
feed.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has unveiled new additions to its Llama family of large language models, including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, which Zuckerberg claims will put open-source AI in the driving seat. These models are available through Meta AI assistant and are designed for enhanced reasoning skills and various tasks. Meta is also working on a larger model, Llama 4 Behemoth, intended to be the "highest performing base model in the world."

feed.title: Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA) Ships 30M Edge AI Chips and Pushes Forward in Generative AI Processing
feed.summary: Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA) has shipped 30 million edge AI chips and is making significant advancements in generative AI processing. The company's focus on edge generative AI technology and its third-generation CVflow AI accelerator highlights its leading position as a supplier of edge AI systems-on-chip, supporting GenAI models from 0.5 to 34 billion parameters. This news comes amidst China's efforts to foster open-source AI models, challenging US dominance in AI innovation and expanding access to AI in developing markets.

feed.title: Aehr Test Systems Reports Fiscal 2025 Third Quarter Revenue Growth and Solid Bookings and Backlog
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems announced its financial results for the third quarter of fiscal 2025, reporting a significant increase in net revenue to $18.3 million, up from $7.6 million in the prior year. The company highlighted strong bookings of $24.1 million and effective backlog of $21.8 million, driven by expansion into new markets like AI processors, gallium nitride (GaN) semiconductors, and hard disk drive components, diversifying revenue beyond silicon carbide. Despite strong performance and new market penetration, Aehr temporarily withdrew its fiscal 2025 guidance due to uncertainty regarding tariff announcements.

feed.title: The 25 Hottest AI Companies For Data Center And Edge: The 2025 CRN AI 100
feed.summary: This article identifies the 25 hottest AI companies for data center and edge computing in 2025, according to CRN. These companies, ranging from tech giants like Cisco, Intel, Dell, and HPE to niche players and startups, are driving AI innovation with specialized hardware, software, and new AI agents. The piece highlights how these firms are transforming enterprise IT environments to handle AIOps and data management, and are poised to capitalize on the projected $749 billion spending on AI-supporting technologies by 2028.

feed.title: Frontier sets fluid dynamics speed record, cutting simulation time from 38.5 to 1.5 hours on AMD GPUs
feed.summary: Ansys, using its Fluent software on the Frontier supercomputer with AMD Instinct GPUs, has drastically reduced the simulation time for complex turbine systems. By leveraging 1,024 Instinct MI250X GPUs instead of over 3,700 CPU cores, the company cut a 2.2-billion-cell axial turbine stator simulation from 38.5 hours to just 1.5 hours. This breakthrough in computational fluid dynamics (CFD) offers significant advantages for industrial design, speeding up iterations and providing more accurate performance forecasts for various applications, including those for small and mid-sized businesses.

feed.title: Semiconductors Leading Nasdaq's Bounce-Back: ETFs in Focus
feed.summary: The Nasdaq saw a significant single-day surge led by semiconductor stocks after President Trump announced a temporary reduction in tariffs. This rebound occurred despite earlier market corrections triggered by initial tariff announcements. The article highlights the strong performance of semiconductor ETFs and specific chip stocks, attributing some of this strength to intensifying competition and advancements in AI models, while also acknowledging potential challenges for leading companies like NVIDIA.

feed.title: Tigress Financial raises Intel stock price target to $52 on AI growth
feed.summary: Tigress Financial Partners has increased its price target for Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) to $52, maintaining a Buy rating due to the company's AI innovation, strategic partnerships, and manufacturing expansion. Intel's return to profitability in Q3, driven by AI accelerator demand and an improved product mix, plus supportive U.S. policies, are expected to fuel multiyear growth. The stock has seen a 75.4% return over the past year and trades at a P/E ratio of 842.35, with 11 analysts revising earnings upwards for the upcoming period.

feed.title: Ten things to know about the European Union's new product liability directive
feed.summary: The European Union has passed a new product liability directive (PLD) which significantly expands the scope of product liability laws, impacting manufacturers of both consumer products and digital goods like software and AI. The directive introduces changes such as a shift in the burden of proof, extended liability periods, and new categories for recoverable damages, all aimed at modernizing product liability for the digital age and increasing consumer protection. Businesses operating in Europe face increased litigation risks and costs, with member states obligated to implement these changes into national law by December 2026.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: AMD Q3 2025 beats forecasts, stock drops
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported record Q3 2025 revenue of $9.2 billion and an EPS of $1.20, significantly exceeding Wall Street forecasts. Despite strong financial results and optimistic Q4 guidance, the company's stock experienced a 3.67% drop in after-hours trading, signaling investor concerns about future growth prospects. AMD emphasized its strategic focus on AI and data center technologies, including major partnerships with OpenAI and Oracle for its next-gen MI450 series GPUs, which are expected to drive substantial revenue growth in the coming years.

feed.title: NVIDIA to Manufacture American-Made AI Supercomputers in US for First Time
feed.summary: NVIDIA is collaborating with manufacturing partners to produce AI supercomputers entirely within the U.S. for the first time. The company is commissioning over a million square feet of manufacturing space in Arizona and Texas to build and test NVIDIA Blackwell chips and AI supercomputers, aiming to complete up to half a trillion dollars of AI infrastructure in the US within four years. This initiative is expected to create hundreds of thousands of jobs and enhance supply chain resiliency while meeting the growing demand for AI technology.

feed.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Says It Will Build Up to $500 Billion of AI Gear in US
feed.summary: Nvidia plans to invest up to $500 billion in AI infrastructure in the US over the next four years through strategic manufacturing partnerships. This initiative includes producing Blackwell AI chips at TSMC's new Phoenix plant, building supercomputer manufacturing facilities in Texas with Foxconn and Wistron, and collaborating with Amkor Technology and Siliconware Precision Industries for packaging and testing in Arizona. The company aims to strengthen its dominant position in the AI chip market.

feed.title: HP Holiday Gift Guide 2025: Top Tech Gifts for Everyone | HP® Tech Takes SEO
feed.summary: HP's 2025 Holiday Gift Guide features a wide range of tech products, including desktops, laptops, printers, monitors, and accessories, designed to suit various needs and budgets. The guide highlights specific HP and OMEN models, detailing their features and ideal users, from serious gamers and creative professionals to students and home office workers. However, the article suggests it's time to consider booking profits, citing concerns about gold's sensitivity to macro factors, RGLD's inconsistent price action compared to gold, and a shrinking P/E ratio indicating limited future upside. feed.title: Haoxin Holdings debuts on Nasdaq at $4.75, IPO priced at $4.00

feed.title: After Altera, is Intel preparing to sell Mobileye?
feed.summary: Intel's recent sale of a 51% stake in its Altera unit for $4.46 billion under new CEO Lip-Bu Tan signals a broader strategy to shed non-core assets and refocus. This divestment, though a financial loss, provides crucial cash for Intel's recovery. Attention is now shifting to Mobileye, which analysts believe could be Intel's next divestment, allowing the company to concentrate on core operations like custom silicon, AI, and foundry services.

feed.title: AMD: Lowering Our Fair Value Estimate Due to China Restrictions and PC Concerns
feed.summary: Morningstar has lowered its fair value estimate for AMD to $120 per share from $140 due to new U.S. restrictions on AI chip exports to China and concerns about PC market performance. These factors led to reduced revenue estimates for AMD's AI GPU and PC CPU segments in 2025 and 2029, even though shares appear undervalued and AMD is expected to remain a winner in PC CPUs and gain some AI market share.

feed.title: Cisco Recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Data Center Switching
feed.summary: Cisco has been recognized as a Leader in Gartner's inaugural Magic Quadrant for Data Center Switching, highlighting its strength in "Ability to Execute" and "Completeness of Vision" in the evolving, AI-driven data center landscape. The company's innovation across its portfolio includes high-performance Nexus 9000 switches and 8000 Series routers, simplified operations with Nexus Dashboard, reimagined network operations through Nexus Hyperfabric, and integrated security via N9300 Series Smart Switches and Hypershield. Cisco continues to focus on flexible, scalable, and secure networking solutions to meet the demands of advanced applications like AI/ML.

feed.title: Taiwan firms bidding for AMD’s server assets
feed.summary: Taiwanese firms Compal Electronics, Wiwynn Corp, and US company Jabil Inc are preparing to submit revised bids for AMD's AI server assembly plants, valued between US$3 billion and US$4 billion. These plants, inherited from AMD's acquisition of ZT Systems, are attractive due to their 1,500 skilled employees and align with the trend of Taiwanese companies building facilities in the US to mitigate potential tariffs. AMD aims to finalize the sale by the end of the second quarter, enabling it to avoid competing with its customers in manufacturing.

feed.title: Europe FPGA Market Size, Share & Trends, 2033
feed.summary: The Europe Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) market, valued at USD 2.24 billion in 2024, is projected to reach USD 5 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.34%. This growth is primarily driven by the expanding adoption of FPGAs in automotive electronics, 5G networks, and AI/machine learning applications, despite challenges like high development costs and design complexity. Germany currently dominates the market, while France and the UK show significant growth potential due to thriving tech and innovation sectors.

feed.title: Nvidia stock dives after chipmaker discloses $5.5 billion hit from 'surprise' China chip controls
feed.summary: Nvidia's stock dropped almost 7% after the company revealed a $5.5 billion hit due to new US government restrictions on its semiconductor exports to China. The restrictions, which require a special license for H20 chips designed for the Chinese market, effectively act as a ban, surprising Wall Street given previous reports of a potential rollback of such measures. Other chipmakers like AMD also saw declines, with analysts questioning the logic of the H20 ban since its performance is considered low compared to existing Chinese alternatives.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. finishes 2nd fab
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has completed construction of its second fab in North Phoenix ahead of schedule, driven by high demand for AI-related chips. The new facility will use 3-nanometer process technology, with production expected to begin sooner than the originally planned 2028. Additionally, TSMC plans to break ground on a third fab for 2-nanometer and A16 technologies later this year, pending permit approval.

feed.title: Cadence Advances AI in the Cloud with Industry-First DDR5 12.8Gbps MRDIMM Gen2 Memory IP System Solution
feed.summary: Cadence announced the industry's first DDR5 12.8Gbps MRDIMM Gen2 memory IP system solution on the TSMC N3 process, designed to meet the increasing memory bandwidth demands of AI processing in data centers and enterprise applications. This new solution features a high-performance PHY and controller, validated in hardware to achieve 12.8Gbps data rates, effectively doubling bandwidth with current DDR5 6400Mbps DRAM parts. The IP system aims to provide a significant performance advantage for advanced SoCs and chiplets, with multiple customer engagements already underway.

feed.title: AT&S sees no immediate tariff impact, keeping close watch on supply chain shifts

However, the company is closely monitoring geopolitical shifts, especially as major players like TSMC and Amkor establish US operations, which could eventually require AT&S to ship directly to the US and expose it to future tariff risks. The Kulim plant, which has started high-volume manufacturing of IC substrates for AMD, is expected to be a significant growth driver for AT&S, aiming to make it a top three IC substrate supplier globally.

However, the article also suggests that other AI stocks might offer greater returns in a shorter timeframe, directing readers to a report on a cheaper AI stock.

feed.title: Chipmakers AMD, GlobalFoundries and Cirrus Logic post solid earnings and guidance, easing tariff fears
feed.summary: U.S. chipmakers AMD, GlobalFoundries, and Cirrus Logic reported strong quarterly earnings and optimistic guidance, easing concerns about the impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs. Despite challenges like new export regulations for AI chips to China affecting AMD, and a slowdown in the smartphone market for GlobalFoundries, all three companies expressed confidence in their ability to navigate current market conditions. GlobalFoundries, in particular, anticipates benefiting from tariffs on foreign-made chips, projecting increased demand for its U.S.-manufactured products.

The company's solutions, including the new Arias 6 PCIe Smart Gearbox, Scorpio P-Series Smart Fabric Switches, and Aries 6 PCIe/CXL Smart DSP Retimers, address the demand for higher bandwidth and lower latency interconnects driven by advanced AI workloads.

feed.title: AMD Earnings: Strong PC Processor Revenue Is Encouraging

Morningstar maintains its $120 fair value estimate but raised the Uncertainty Rating to Very High due to tariff and geopolitical concerns despite the positive earnings.

TSM faces greater geopolitical, cost, and supply-chain risks, reflected in its recent downward earnings revisions and increased capital spending.

feed.title: Winning the AI race: Strengthening U.S. capabilities in computing and innovation

He highlighted the need for federal support in expanding datacenters and electrical grids, training a skilled AI workforce, and investing in research and development. The article stresses that winning the AI race requires a robust ecosystem including both private sector efforts and government policies, focusing on both technological advancement and widespread adoption of AI across all sectors.

The GPU Droplets are available in DigitalOcean's Toronto data centers and are designed for simple setup and integration with the full DigitalOcean product portfolio.

feed.title: Intel spars with EU regulators over $421.4 million antitrust fine

The fine stems from payments made by Intel to HP, Acer, and Lenovo to delay or halt rival products between 2002 and 2006. Intel's lawyer argued that these were "narrow, tactical moves" and did not represent a market-wide foreclosure strategy, while the EU watchdog maintains the fine is appropriate and not disproportionate.

feed.title: A New Path! US Capital to Jointly Build Entire Rare Earth Industry Chain in Saudi Arabia

This collaboration aims to diversify the global rare earth supply chain, leveraging Saudi Arabia's resources and MP Materials' expertise. The initiative is part of broader US investment in Saudi Arabia, with the goal of strengthening critical mineral supply chains amidst growing demand for advanced technologies.

feed.title: Clean energy stocks shine bright as improved trade outlook sparks rally

feed.title: Sify launches pay-per-use colocation pricing at their NVIDIA-certified AI data centre campuses
feed.summary: Sify Technologies is introducing a pay-per-use colocation pricing model for its NVIDIA-certified AI data centers to remove cost barriers for AI adoption. This new model will bill companies for colocation charges based on GPU usage, rather than fixed costs, making it easier for new-age AI cloud service providers and global enterprises to deploy their infrastructure in India. The initiative targets neo-cloud firms and will be available at Sify's hyperscale data centers in Chennai, Noida, and Navi Mumbai.

feed.title: How Will Analog Devices Stock React To Q2 Earnings?
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) is projected to report strong Q2 FY'25 earnings with anticipated revenue of $2.51 billion and EPS of $1.70, driven by improving demand in industrial, automotive, and data center sectors. Historically, ADI has shown a slightly higher probability of positive one-day post-earnings returns, particularly over the last three years. The article also discusses strategies for understanding post-earnings stock reactions, including correlations between short-term and medium-term returns and peer performance.

feed.title: Mizuho Raises Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Target to $135, Reiterates Outperform
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to $135 from $117, maintaining an "Outperform" rating, following an artificial intelligence server supply chain call. The article also discusses the broader context of AI's energy demands, mentioning former US President Donald Trump's push for coal-fired plants to meet this need, despite the tech industry's focus on renewable energy and natural gas. AMD is highlighted as a significant AI stock, ranking 9th on Wall Street's radar, with a strong presence in semiconductors, processors, and GPUs for AI.

feed.title: Efficiency upgrade for OLED screens: A route to blue PHOLED longevity
feed.summary: Researchers at the University of Michigan have developed a blue phosphorescent OLED (PHOLED) that matches the longevity and efficiency of existing green PHOLEDs. This breakthrough addresses a long-standing challenge in OLED technology, where blue OLEDs typically use less efficient fluorescent methods, limiting overall display performance. The team achieved this by implementing design innovations, including a special coating on the negative electrode to enhance energy conversion into blue light and a tandem OLED structure to reduce stress on light-emitting layers.

feed.title: Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, TI join call for chip tariff exemptions ...
feed.summary: US chip giants Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, and Texas Instruments have asked the US Department of Commerce for semiconductor import tariff exemptions or lighter burdens. Their submissions are a response to a "Section 232" investigation into semiconductor imports, coming after TSMC also appealed to the Trump administration. The companies, while supporting reshoring, warn that poorly designed tariffs could harm US interests and the complex semiconductor supply chain.

feed.title: KEPCO to invest $53.1 bil. by 2038 to expand power supply infrastructure for advanced industries
feed.summary: Korea Electric Power Corp. (KEPCO) announced a plan to invest $53.1 billion by 2038 to enhance power supply infrastructure for key industries like semiconductors and improve its electricity transmission system. This investment includes building a substation in a Yongin industrial cluster to power AI memory chip production and establishing advanced high-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems. KEPCO projects this initiative will generate significant economic impact, valued at approximately 134 trillion won, and create 480,000 jobs.

feed.title: Semiconductor ETF options show caution ahead of Nvidia results
feed.summary: Traders in the options market are showing caution ahead of Nvidia's earnings report, with significant defensive options contracts being traded on the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). This reflects concerns about potential industry-wide volatility, even as options activity on Nvidia itself is more mixed, with some investors selling calls to monetize elevated premiums. The heightened activity underscores investor interest and some apprehension following an earlier strong market run.

feed.title: Semiconductor ETF options show caution ahead of Nvidia results
feed.summary: Options traders are showing caution ahead of Nvidia's earnings report, with significant defensive trading seen in the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). Put options on the SMH ETF have been heavily traded, suggesting concerns about sector-wide volatility. While some investors are hedging in SMH, others are selling Nvidia options to capitalize on heightened volatility expectations.

feed.title: Arrive AI obtains $40M for autonomous deliveries to smart mailboxes
feed.summary: Arrive AI Inc. has secured $40 million in funding from Streeterville Capital LLC for its autonomous delivery network, which leverages AI-powered smart mailboxes called "Arrive Points." The company's platform aims to enable secure and efficient last-mile deliveries by various autonomous and human methods. This funding coincides with Arrive AI's public debut on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "ARAI," with its stock opening at $40.

feed.title: Nvidia delivers another earnings and revenue beat on rampant data center growth
feed.summary: Nvidia's shares rose almost 5% after reporting better-than-expected earnings and revenue for the first quarter, driven by a 73% year-over-year growth in its data center business. Despite missing guidance due to U.S. export restrictions on advanced chips to China, the company's strong performance in AI infrastructure and other sectors, including gaming and automotive, reassured investors. CEO Jensen Huang acknowledged the closure of the Chinese AI chip market for U.S. industry but emphasized the overwhelming global demand for Nvidia's AI solutions.

feed.title: Semiconductor Market Braces for Nvidia Earnings Amid Caution
feed.summary: The semiconductor market is showing caution ahead of Nvidia's earnings report on May 28, with significant trading in protective put options on semiconductor ETFs, indicating investor nervousness about potential volatility. Despite this, options activity on Nvidia itself is mixed, with some traders selling options, suggesting a bet against extreme post-earnings price swings. Nvidia's results are expected to significantly influence the semiconductor sector, as it is the last of the "Magnificent Seven" to report this earnings period.

feed.title: Notable ETF Outflow Detected - TQQQ, AMD, ADBE, AMGN
feed.summary: The ProShares UltraPro QQQ (TQQQ) ETF experienced a significant outflow of approximately $610.1 million, marking a 2.4% decrease week-over-week. This outflow indicates a reduction in units, which can lead to the selling of underlying holdings like Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD), Adobe Inc (ADBE), and Amgen Inc (AMGN). The article also highlights TQQQ's recent price performance, noting its 52-week range and current trading price compared to its 200-day moving average.

feed.title: ASE Announces FOCoS-Bridge With TSV; Latest Package Technology Reduces Power Loss by 3x for Next-Generation AI and HPC Applications
feed.summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering, Inc. (ASE) introduced its new Fan-Out Chip-on-Substrate-Bridge (FOCoS-Bridge) with Through Silicon Via (TSV) technology to address the increasing demands for bandwidth and energy efficiency in AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) applications. This innovation, part of ASE's VIPack™ platform, significantly reduces power loss by creating a shorter delivery path and enhancing thermal dissipation. The FOCoS-Bridge with TSV achieved a 72% reduction in resistance and 50% in inductance compared to the previous FOCoS-Bridge, making it crucial for sustainable, high-performance computing architectures.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Inc.'s (NASDAQ:GFS) Price Is Out Of Tune With Revenues
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries Inc.'s (NASDAQ:GFS) current price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of 3x is considered moderate within the Semiconductor industry, despite the company's recent revenue decline of 4.4% and an overall 4.5% drop over the last three years. Analysts forecast a modest 7.8% annual revenue growth for GlobalFoundries over the next three years, significantly lower than the industry's projected 24% growth. This disparity raises questions about why the market is not negatively impacting GFS's P/S ratio more, potentially indicating an overvaluation given its limited growth expectations.

feed.title: Synopsys pulls full-year guidance, citing new China export restrictions
feed.summary: Synopsys has withdrawn its full fiscal year guidance after receiving a letter from the U.S. Commerce Department imposing new restrictions on sales of its chip design software to China. This decision comes despite CEO Sassine Ghazi previously downplaying reports of such restrictions, and follows a period of slowing growth for the company in China, which accounts for about 10% of its quarterly revenue. Rival Cadence also reported receiving a similar letter regarding export licenses for its software due to "military end use" concerns.

feed.title: CHT ITG delivers sustainable performance cloud with AMD
feed.summary: Chunghwa Telecom Information Technology Group (CHT ITG) has successfully improved its cloud service performance and met environmental goals by deploying 3rd Gen AMD EPYC™ CPUs. This strategic move resulted in a 1.6x reduction in data center space, optimized hardware costs, and reduced energy consumption, aligning with ESG targets. The high core density and performance of AMD EPYC processors proved ideal for CHT ITG's diverse cloud workloads, including evolving AI applications, leading to enhanced service offerings and a roadmap for future expansion with upcoming generations of AMD EPYC CPUs.

feed.title: Salesforce Earnings: Stock Attractive With Agentforce Momentum Building
feed.summary: Salesforce reported strong fiscal first-quarter results, exceeding guidance with 8% revenue growth to $9.83 billion, primarily driven by Data Cloud and Agentforce. The company raised its fair value estimate to $325 per share due to positive results and increased full-year guidance, despite uncertainties from the Informatica deal. Agentforce, generating $100 million in ARR, continues to build momentum, making Salesforce shares attractive.

feed.title: Alliance entertainment executive chairman Bruce Ogilvie buys $5,400 in stock
feed.summary: Alliance Entertainment Holding Corp's Executive Chairman, Bruce A. Ogilvie Jr., recently invested $5,400 in the company's common stock and acquired numerous warrants. These transactions reflect his continued commitment to the company, whose stock has declined by 44% in the last six months but recently returned to profitability in Q3 FY25 with a net income of $1.9 million. The company is also expanding its distribution through strategic partnerships and a focus on direct-to-consumer fulfillment.

feed.title: Malaysia's delicate balancing act
feed.summary: Malaysia is facing increasing pressure to abandon its neutral stance amidst the escalating US-China tech war, particularly concerning semiconductor supply chains. The US's efforts to reshore chip production are reconfiguring the global industry, impacting Malaysia, which is a significant semiconductor exporter. A recent incident involving Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching and Huawei’s Ascend chips highlighted the difficulties Malaysia faces in maintaining its neutrality given geopolitical tensions.

feed.title: Synopsys suspends China sales; Ansys merger gets FTC nod
feed.summary: Synopsys has suspended all sales to China due to new trade restrictions from the Trump administration, impacting a market that accounts for about 10% of its revenues. Concurrently, the Federal Trade Commission has conditionally approved Synopsys' $35 billion acquisition of Ansys, requiring divestment of certain assets—specifically optical software tools, photonic software tools, and power consumption analysis tools—to Keysight Technologies due to anticompetitive concerns. This follows similar regulatory hurdles in the European Union, while the China sales suspension may complicate the Ansys acquisition's approval in China.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Announces $16B U.S. Investment to Reshore Essential Chip Manufacturing and Accelerate AI Growth
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) announced a $16 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities across its facilities in New York and Vermont. This strategic investment is backed by major tech companies like Apple, SpaceX, AMD, Qualcomm, NXP, and GM, and aims to reshore essential chip manufacturing to the U.S., accelerating innovation in AI-enabling and power-efficient technologies. The investment supports the explosive growth in AI and strengthens the domestic semiconductor supply chain and leadership.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries boosts investment plans to $16 billion, with research focus
authors: Max A. Cherney and Stephen Nellis
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries announced plans to increase its investment to $16 billion, allocating an additional $1 billion to capital spending and $3 billion to research in emerging chip technologies. This expansion is driven by the booming artificial intelligence hardware market and is expected to support factory expansions in New York and Vermont, as well as significant research in chip packaging, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride. The company is also collaborating with the Trump administration to fortify the U.S. chip manufacturing supply chain.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries will invest $16 billion in facilities in New York and Essex Junction VT
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries announced a $16 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities in New York and Essex Junction, VT. This investment responds to the growing demand for next-generation semiconductors driven by artificial intelligence and aims to onshore critical supply chain components. The company is collaborating with major technology firms like Apple, SpaceX, and AMD to support U.S.-made chip production and strengthen supply chain resilience.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries commits $16B to expand New York, Vermont facilities
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries is investing an additional $3 billion to expand its semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities in Malta, New York, and Essex Junction, Vermont, bringing its total commitment to $16 billion. This expansion, driven by diversifying supply chains due to potential tariffs and increasing AI demand, focuses on packaging innovation, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride technologies. The company is collaborating with major firms like Apple and General Motors to reshore production in the U.S.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries pledges $16bn investment to reshore chip manufacturing at New York and Vermont fabs
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) has committed an additional $3 billion investment to enhance semiconductor manufacturing at its New York and Vermont facilities, increasing its total US investment to $16 billion. This expansion supports advanced R&D in packaging innovation, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride technologies, with collaborations including major companies like Apple, AMD, and Qualcomm. The investment is partly supported by $1.5 billion from the CHIPS and Science Act funding and New York State Green CHIPS Program, aiming to strengthen domestic semiconductor supply chains despite former President Trump's past criticisms of the CHIPS Act.

feed.title: Dell Technologies vs. HPQ: Which PC Stock Has More Growth Potential?
feed.summary: This article compares Dell Technologies (DELL) and HP (HPQ) as investments in the growing PC market, which is projected to reach $344.13 billion by 2030. It highlights DELL's strong AI infrastructure, enterprise focus, and robust earnings outlook, especially with its AI PCs and partnerships with NVIDIA and Microsoft. HPQ is also expanding its AI PC portfolio but faces risks from its heavy reliance on Chinese manufacturing amid potential tariff escalations.

feed.title: Test and Measurement: DoJ orders Keysight to shed Spirent’s channel emulation
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice has mandated that Keysight Technologies divest Spirent Communications' channel emulation capabilities to Viavi Solutions as part of its acquisition of Spirent. This is in addition to the previously agreed divestment of Spirent's high-speed Ethernet and network security assets, ensuring competitive balance in the test and measurement market. The DoJ cited concerns about market concentration in high-speed Ethernet testing, network security testing, and RF channel emulators if Keysight were to acquire these Spirent assets.

feed.title: Leverage Shares 2X Long RTX Daily ETF (RTXG) Stock Price, News, Quote & History
feed.summary: This page provides detailed information on the Leverage Shares 2X Long RTX Daily ETF (RTXG), including its current stock price, historical data, and performance metrics. The ETF aims to provide 200% daily leveraged exposure to the price of RTX. It also lists related tickers and various financial data points.

feed.title: Foley Represents Mayville Engineering Company in Acquisition of Accu-Fab
feed.summary: Foley & Lardner LLP represented Mayville Engineering Company (MEC) in its definitive agreement to acquire Accu-Fab, LLC from Tide Rock for $140.5 million. This acquisition supports MEC’s strategic goal of expanding into high-growth adjacent end-markets, including critical power infrastructure, data centers, and renewable energy. The transaction, expected to close in Q3 2025, aims to enhance MEC's commercial reach, achieve revenue synergies, and generate operational efficiencies.

feed.title: Cathie Wood Snaps Up Amazon and AMD Stocks but Offloads Palantir and CoreWeave
feed.summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs recently acquired significant shares of Amazon (AMZN) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), signaling confidence in these tech giants' growth potential despite market concerns. Conversely, ARK offloaded shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and CoreWeave (CRWV), with analysts noting valuation concerns for both. Wood also made other notable purchases including Guardant Health Inc. (GH) and 10X Genomics Inc. (TXG).
